LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE MINI-MINUTES THIS DATE FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-20-2021 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-16-2021 Continued To Jun-09-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-18-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant San Francisco Bay Area Rapid Transit District's demurrer to plaintiff's first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff seeks to hold Defendant liable for damages for personal injuries he sustained when, as he was leaving a BART station, he was assaulted by other passengers whom he criticized for fare evasion. He alleges two different theories. Neither has merit.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
